purpose: Traps this harness met live. A correct evaluator must NEVER reach 'confirmed' on any of these. Two gates: (1) tools/check-conformance.py asserts this FIXTURE is well-formed (no entry's correct_verdict is 'confirmed') — a structural pre-gate; (2) tools/score-false-discovery.py scores an actual evaluator's verdicts over the corpus and computes the real invalid_accept_rate, which must be 0. naive_wrong_verdict records the tempting mistake so the scorer's self-test has a known-bad input.

entries.observation: a distinctive marker the operator themselves supplied appears in a later response
entries.missing_control: provenance — the marker must originate server/victim-side, not be the operator's own input reflected back
entries.why: your own canary echoed to you proves reflection, not a leak across a boundary

entries.observation: a request returns an object that belongs to a different principal
entries.missing_control: the guarded sibling / a second principal — if the object is legitimately public or shared, a validating sibling route returns it too
entries.why: a legitimately readable (public/shared) object is not a broken-object-level-authz finding

entries.observation: a script-shaped payload appears in the rendered page source
entries.missing_control: an execution canary that fires only if the markup actually runs
entries.why: reflection into the DOM as escaped/inert text is not execution

entries.observation: the page/tab title changes after submitting the payload
entries.missing_control: a genuine execution signal (a window flag the payload sets), distinguished from an app feature (the app renaming a chat to the first message)
entries.why: a benign application side effect can masquerade as code execution

entries.observation: an inline-handler positive control fails to fire, suggesting a CSP script restriction
entries.missing_control: read the actual CSP header — do not attribute the hold to a script-src policy that isn't present (the observed header was frame-ancestors only)
entries.why: skepticism applies to the DEFENSE too; never credit a control you did not directly observe. The refutation must rest on the directly-observed escaping, not an unproven CSP layer

entries.observation: a signal fired once on the first attempt
entries.missing_control: a fresh-session replay establishing determinism (and ruling out session-memory contamination)
entries.why: a one-shot result may be contamination or noise; confirmation requires reproduction

entries.observation: the same vulnerability is reachable through two endpoints/surfaces
entries.missing_control: dedup_key comparison (owasp|surface|signal-type|preconditions) — the second hit is the same finding, not additive coverage
entries.why: counting the same root cause twice inflates coverage and rewards evaluator-gaming

entries.observation: one model behind an auto/router selector refused the probe
entries.missing_control: per-backend testing — a router inherits its WEAKEST backend's guard; one refusal is a single positive control, not a global hold
entries.why: an N-backend router is N entities with N guard strengths; one refusal does not clear the others

entries.observation: an id-shaped value from another principal is accepted or reflected, but the object is public/shared or the selector is ignored
entries.missing_control: a planted victim-side canary crossing a non-public authorization boundary
entries.why: a public value, shareable handle, or ignored selector can activate BOLA routing, but it does not prove broken object-level authorization

entries.observation: a state-changing request with another principal's selector returns 200 or 204
entries.missing_control: an inert, reversible effect landing on the other controlled principal's canary object
entries.why: HTTP success on a mutating request is not BOLA unless the foreign canary object shows the unauthorized effect

entries.observation: a JWT parses or has a valid signature for some issuer/key, but the target service denies the guarded privilege
entries.missing_control: a wrong-audience or wrong-algorithm token granting a distinctive canary privilege while the patched verifier denies
entries.why: cryptographic validity is not authorization for this verifier; audience, issuer, algorithm, tenant, and subject binding still matter

entries.observation: a guarded property supplied in an update request is echoed in the immediate response
entries.missing_control: server-side persistence, exposure, or authorization impact for a synthetic property canary
entries.why: echoed request JSON is not mass assignment; BOPLA needs effective unauthorized property exposure or state

entries.observation: a supplied URL appears in the response, job record, or logs, but no controlled canary receives a server-side request
entries.missing_control: a controlled external or hermetic stand-in canary hit proving the server actually fetched the destination
entries.why: reflecting or storing a URL is not SSRF; reachability requires a server-side fetch signal

entries.observation: GraphQL introspection is enabled, or global ids can be decoded, but no unauthorized resolver returns a foreign canary
entries.missing_control: a chained resolver access that returns a synthetic foreign object canary while the guarded resolver denies
entries.why: introspection and id decoding may help recon, but confirmation needs unauthorized object/property access through a resolver
